findings.message: DB_COUNT query filters `title NOT LIKE 'Cron: %'` but SPEC.md doesn't document that getTasksCompletedToday filters cron tasks. If the production function omits that filter (or filters differently), RENDERED != DB_COUNT and the AC fails even when Fix 7 is correct. Spec should either disclose the existing noise filter or AC-D8 should drop the cron predicate to match the function as written.

Bug 1 check `<html [^>]*class="[^"]*\bdark\b` only catches server-rendered dark class. If next-themes applies .dark via client hydration, the server HTML stays neutral and this assertion silently passes regardless of enableSystem. Source-level AC-D1 still catches the actual fix, so coverage is intact, but AC-D7's Bug 1 check is largely a tautology.

Spec commits to 8-item featured allowlist (graphify, invoicing, gws-meta-workflows, browser, cold-email, content-strategy, seo, m2c1-worker) but AC-D3 only verifies 4 names (graphify, invoicing, gws-meta-workflows, cold-email). Codexer could ship a 4-item list and pass. Either extend AC-D3 to require all 8 or downgrade SPEC.md to soft 'initial set'.

AC-D4 only checks markup substrings (Dialog import, button/Trigger/onClick within 200 chars, search input present). It does not verify the modal actually opens on click or that the search filter narrows the list. A non-functional Dialog wired to a no-op trigger would pass. Acceptable for this scope but worth noting.

Staging AC verifies the overflow button is interactive markup but doesn't open the modal (server render only). The 'cannot reach any skill past the first ten' contract isn't end-to-end verified in HTML. Playwright would be the right tool but is out of scope here.

ac_failure_simulation.would_fail_if: Codexer leaves enableSystem on ThemeProvider, or accidentally drops defaultTheme="light" / attribute="class" during the edit — any of the three chained checks under set -e exits non-zero.

Codexer keeps `fs.readdirSync(agentsDir)` raw (no second-arg options) or wraps it in something that doesn't call safeReadDir(agentsDir) — the regex/substring assertions in the readAgentListFromFilesystem body raise AssertionError.

Codexer skips the user-disk source and only edits bus-skill merge logic — readUserSkillsFromDisk, os.homedir(), .claude/skills path, SKILL.md, LAUNCHER_FEATURED, or any of {graphify, invoicing, gws-meta-workflows, cold-email} missing from source = assert trips.

Codexer leaves the overflow as `<span data-testid=launcher-skill-more>` with no button/onClick/DialogTrigger nearby, omits Dialog import, or fails to add a search input — one of the three assertions trips.
Codexer leaves setUTCHours in getTasksCompletedToday body or substitutes a different IANA timezone (e.g., uses Date.now() local) — body inspection assertion trips.
Any TypeScript error from the launcher modal wiring (missing prop, wrong Dialog import path, `any` violation, unused prop in <ClaudeCodeLauncher>) — next build exits non-zero.

.DS_Store leaks into rendered launcher tiles, html element gets server-rendered .dark class, invoicing or graphify chip missing from rendered launcher, or overflow trigger renders as a span instead of a button — any of the four grep/python assertions fails.

Rendered Velocity card number doesn't match sqlite count using independently computed PT-midnight-UTC boundary — wrong tz math, wrong offset handling around DST, wrong noise-filter, or off-by-one would all surface as RENDERED != DB_COUNT. Also fails (correctly) if the regex can't locate the velocity number at all.
